Monitoring airport activity

Trebor Banstetter September-October 2006
Other

Explores the impact of AirTran Airways' bid to operate a mini-hub at Dallas-Fort Worth Airport and its effect on fares, traffic demand, and competition with American Airlines using…

Open

Aron Pilhofer January February 2006
Other

This article provides a step-by-step guide for using TortoiseSVN to create repository checkouts, add documents, and synchronize files across multiple computers in a newsroom enviro…

AFTER A HURRICANE Probing FEMA's chronic illness

John Maines November-December 2005
Other

John Maines investigates FEMA's ineffective response to hurricanes, showcasing the agency's struggles with fraud, waste, and mismanagement in disaster relief efforts.

Tech tip PDF to text using XPDF

Derek Willis July-August 2004
Other

Xpdf is a free command-line utility that allows journalists to easily extract text and tables from PDF files and convert them to usable formats like Excel, saving significant time …

PEACE CORPS Analysis exposes attacks on volunteers

Russell Carollo June 2004
Other

An investigation uncovers the increasing rate of violent attacks, including rapes, against Peace Corps volunteers, predominantly young women serving overseas.

Following the riverboat pilots

Jeffrey Meitrodt March-April 2002
Other

Investigation into Louisiana's archaic system for selecting and regulating Mississippi River pilots, revealing nepotism and other systemic issues.

Poor airport security

Matt Carroll February 2002
Other

Following the September 11 attacks, the investigation focused on how hijackers bypassed security at Logan International Airport, exposing the Massachusetts Port Authority as a patr…

Coaching for dollars

Kara Morrison November 1997
Other

This issue highlights investigative reporting in sports, focusing on coaching salaries in Texas high schools and NCAA's role in college sports finances. It discusses the AP's local…

Work is killing me! Lax labor laws imperil Texas teens

Sarah Baxter June/July 1993
Other

Nancy Stancill uncovered numerous instances of Texas teenagers suffering injuries and long work hours under unenforced child labor laws, prompting legislative action.
